short_description: Download & Save Mobifcuk Videos Easily for Offline Viewing (Browser Extension)
long_description: This extension is built around the specific page shape found on MobiFcuk, where video content lives on root-level article slug pages and playback often shifts into an embedded iframe. Rather than assuming a simple video page, this tool works with the actual page structure — waiting for the iframe handoff and surfacing only the media formats that are grounded in the verified page behavior.

faqs.question: Does this extension work on all MobiFcuk pages?
faqs.answer: It is designed for root-level article slug pages where playback shifts into an iframe. Other page types may not be supported.

faqs.question: What media formats can it download?
faqs.answer: The extension inspects m3u8 playlists and mp4 files that become visible through the iframe handoff workflow.

faqs.question: Is the extension ready for daily use?
faqs.answer: The target is verified and marked as ready, but stale configuration files and a generated stub note mean the release should be treated as a candidate-stage tool rather than a fully polished product.

faqs.question: Do I need an account to use it?
faqs.answer: You can use 3 free downloads without an account. A paid license with email sign-in is required for unlimited use.

faqs.question: Why does the extension mention mobile-ish branding?
faqs.answer: The MobiFcuk name suggests a mobile tone, but the packet does not prove a mobile app. The wording stays descriptive rather than speculative.

why: MobiFcuk pages follow a specific pattern that differs from typical video sites. Instead of a clean video page with direct media links, content lives on root-level article slug pages like /dad-couldnt-resist/. The actual playback often shifts into an embedded iframe before any m3u8 playlist or mp4 file becomes visible. This page structure makes it harder to find and save media using generic downloaders.

This extension is designed around that exact workflow. It understands the root-level slug route shape, waits for the iframe handoff, and inspects only the media sources that are grounded in the verified page behavior. The tool keeps its claims tight — targeting m3u8 and mp4 formats, respecting the mobile-ish brand naming, and maintaining honest caveats about the current readiness stage.
how_it_works: Install the extension from the latest release.
Open MobiFcuk and go to a supported root-level article slug page.
Start playback so the iframe handoff can complete.
Open the popup to inspect available media sources.
Review the detected m3u8 or mp4 options.
Start the download and wait for the export to finish.
tutorial: Install the Mobifcuk Downloader extension from the latest GitHub release.
Navigate to a MobiFcuk page with a root-level article slug, such as /dad-couldnt-resist/.
Allow the page to load fully, including any embedded iframe content.
Start video playback so the iframe handoff can complete and media sources become visible.
Click the extension icon in your browser toolbar to open the popup.
Wait for the popup to detect available media sources from the iframe.
Review the detected m3u8 or mp4 options and choose the one you want.
Click the download button and save the exported file to your device.
supported_formats: Input: m3u8 playlists and mp4 files exposed through iframe playback handoff on MobiFcuk root-level article slug pages
Saved files use MP4 so they are easier to replay on standard media players, move between devices, or archive locally.

troubleshooting: The popup shows no media sources after starting playback. Make sure the iframe has fully loaded and playback has begun. Some pages require a short wait before sources become visible.
The download fails or produces a broken file. Check your internet connection and ensure the source is still available. Some m3u8 playlists require the full stream to be accessible.
The extension does not activate on a MobiFcuk page. Verify you are on a supported root-level article slug page. The extension is designed for pages directly under the site root.
I see an error about stale configuration. The extension is still in a verified-target readiness stage. Updates will address configuration refinements as the project matures.
The download button is grayed out. You may have used your free trial downloads. Sign in with email to check your license status.
